name: semsql
documentation:
# SemSQL: standard SQL views for RDF/OWL ontologies

[![PyPI version](https://badge.fury.io/py/semsql.svg)](https://badge.fury.io/py/semsql)
![](https://github.com/incatools/semantic-sql/workflows/Build/badge.svg)


This project provides a standard collection of SQL tables/views for ontologies, such that you can make queries like this,
to find all terms starting with `Abnormality` in [HPO](https://obofoundry.org/ontology/hp).

```sql
$ sqlite db/hp.db
sqlite> SELECT * FROM rdfs_label_statement WHERE value LIKE 'Abnormality of %';
```

|stanza|subject|predicate|object|value|datatype|language|
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
|HP:0000002|HP:0000002|rdfs:label||Abnormality of body height|xsd:string||
|HP:0000014|HP:0000014|rdfs:label||Abnormality of the bladder|xsd:string||
|HP:0000022|HP:0000022|rdfs:label||Abnormality of male internal genitalia|xsd:string||
|HP:0000032|HP:0000032|rdfs:label||Abnormality of male external genitalia|xsd:string||


Ready-made SQLite3 builds can also be downloaded for any ontology in [OBO](http://obofoundry.org), using URLs such as https://s3.amazonaws.com/bbop-sqlite/hp.db

[relation-graph](https://github.com/balhoff/relation-graph/) is used to pre-generate tables of [entailed edges](https://incatools.github.io/semantic-sql/EntailedEdge/). For example,
all is-a and part-of ancestors of [finger](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002389) in Uberon:

```sql
$ sqlite db/uberon.db
sqlite> SELECT * FROM entailed_edge WHERE subject='UBERON:0002389' and predicate IN ('rdfs:subClassOf', 'BFO:0000050');
```

|subject, predicate, object|
|---|
|UBERON:0002389, BFO:0000050, UBERON:0015212|
|UBERON:0002389, BFO:0000050, UBERON:5002389|
|UBERON:0002389, BFO:0000050, UBERON:5002544|
|UBERON:0002389, rdfs:subClassOf, UBERON:0000061|
|UBERON:0002389, rdfs:subClassOf, UBERON:0000465|
|UBERON:0002389, rdfs:subClassOf, UBERON:0000475|

SQLite provides many advantages

- files can be downloaded and subsequently queried without network latency
- compared to querying a static rdf, owl, or obo file, there is no startup/parse delay
- robust and performant
- excellent support in many languages

Although the focus is on SQLite, this library can also be used for other DBMSs like PostgreSQL, MySQL, Oracle, etc

## Tutorials

- SemSQL: [notebooks/SemanticSQL-Tutorial.ipynb](https://github.com/INCATools/semantic-sql/blob/main/notebooks/SemanticSQL-Tutorial.ipynb)
- Using OAK: [part 7 of OAK tutorial](https://incatools.github.io/ontology-access-kit/intro/tutorial07.html)

## Installation

SemSQL comes with a helper Python library. Use of this is optional. To install:

```bash
pip install semsql
```

## Download ready-made SQLite databases

Pre-generated SQLite database are created weekly for all OBO ontologies and a selection of others (see [ontologies.yaml](https://github.com/INCATools/semantic-sql/blob/main/src/semsql/builder/registry/ontologies.yaml))

To download:

```bash
semsql download obi -o obi.db
```

Or simply download using URL of the form:

- https://s3.amazonaws.com/bbop-sqlite/hp.db

## Attaching databases

If you are using sqlite3, then databases can be attached to facilitate cross-database joins.

For example, many ontologies use ORCID URIs as the object of `dcterms:contributor` and `dcterms:creator` statements, but these are left "dangling". Metadata about these orcids are available in the semsql orcid database instance (derived from [wikidata-orcid-ontology](https://github.com/cthoyt/wikidata-orcid-ontology)), in the [Orcid table](https://incatools.github.io/semantic-sql/Orcid).

You can use [ATTACH DATABASE](https://www.sqlite.org/lang_attach.html) to connect two databases, for example:

```sql
$ sqlite3 db/cl.dl
sqlite> attach 'db/orcid.db' as orcid_db;
sqlite> select * from contributor inner join orcid_db.orcid on (orcid.id=contributor.object) where orcid.label like 'Chris%';
obo:cl.owl|obo:cl.owl|dcterms:contributor|orcid:0000-0002-6601-2165||||orcid:0000-0002-6601-2165|Christopher J. Mungall
CL:0010001|CL:0010001|dcterms:contributor|orcid:0000-0002-6601-2165||||orcid:0000-0002-6601-2165|Christopher J. Mungall
CL:0010002|CL:0010002|dcterms:contributor|orcid:0000-0002-6601-2165||||orcid:0000-0002-6601-2165|Christopher J. Mungall
CL:0010003|CL:0010003|dcterms:contributor|orcid:0000-0002-6601-2165||||orcid:0000-0002-6601-2165|Christopher J. Mungall
CL:0010004|CL:0010004|dcterms:contributor|orcid:0000-0002-6601-2165||||orcid:0000-0002-6601-2165|Christopher J. Mungall
UBERON:0000093|UBERON:0000093|dcterms:contributor|orcid:0000-0002-6601-2165||||orcid:0000-0002-6601-2165|Christopher J. Mungall
UBERON:0000094|UBERON:0000094|dcterms:contributor|orcid:0000-0002-6601-2165||||orcid:0000-0002-6601-2165|Christopher J. Mungall
UBERON:0000095|UBERON:0000095|dcterms:contributor|orcid:0000-0002-6601-2165||||orcid:0000-0002-6601-2165|Christopher J. Mungall
UBERON:0000179|UBERON:0000179|dcterms:contributor|orcid:0000-0002-6601-2165||||orcid:0000-0002-6601-2165|Christopher J. Mungall
UBERON:0000201|UBERON:0000201|dcterms:contributor|orcid:0000-0002-6601-2165||||orcid:0000-0002-6601-2165|Christopher J. Mungall
UBERON:0000202|UBERON:0000202|dcterms:contributor|orcid:0000-0002-6601-2165||||orcid:0000-0002-6601-2165|Christopher J. Mungall
UBERON:0000203|UBERON:0000203|dcterms:contributor|orcid:0000-0002-6601-2165||||orcid:0000-0002-6601-2165|Christopher J. Mungall
UBERON:0000204|UBERON:0000204|dcterms:contributor|orcid:0000-0002-6601-2165||||orcid:0000-0002-6601-2165|Christopher J. Mungall
```

## Creating a SQLite database from an OWL file

There are two protocols for doing this:

1. install build dependencies
2. use Docker

In either case:

- The input MUST be in RDF/XML serialization and have the suffix `.owl`:
- use robot to convert if format is different

We are planning to simplify this process in future.

### 1. Build a SQLite database directly

This requires some basic technical knowledge about how to install things on your machine
and how to put things in your PATH. It does not require Docker.

Requirements:

- [rdftab.rs](https://github.com/ontodev/rdftab.rs)
- [relation-graph](https://github.com/balhoff/relation-graph)

After installing these and putting both `relation-graph` and `rdftab.rs` in your path:

```bash
semsql make foo.db
```

This assumes `foo.owl` is in the same folder

### 2. Use Docker

There are two docker images that can be used:

- ODK
- [semantic-sql](https://hub.docker.com/repository/docker/linkml/semantic-sql)

The ODK image may lag behind

```bash
docker run  -v $PWD:/work -w /work -ti linkml/semantic-sql semsql make foo.db
```

## Schema

See [Schema Documentation](https://incatools.github.io/semantic-sql/)

The [source schema](https://github.com/INCATools/semantic-sql/tree/main/src/semsql/linkml) is in [LinkML](https://linkml.io) - this is then compiled down to SQL Tables and Views

The basic idea is as follows:

There are a small number of "base tables":

* [statements](https://incatools.github.io/semantic-sql/Statements/)
* [prefix](https://incatools.github.io/semantic-sql/Prefix/)
* [entailed_edge](https://incatools.github.io/semantic-sql/EntailedEdge/) - populated by relation-graph

All other tables are actually views (derived tables), and are provided for convenience.

## ORM Layer

A SemSQL relational database can be accessed in exactly the same way as any other SQLdb

For convenience, we provide a Python Object-Relational Mapping (ORM) layer using SQL Alchemy.
This allows for code uchlike the following, which joins [RdfsSubclassOfStatement](https://incatools.github.io/semantic-sql/RdfsSubclassOfStatement) and [existential restrictions](https://incatools.github.io/semantic-sql/OwlSomeValuesFrom):

```python
engine = create_engine(f"sqlite:////path/to/go.db")
SessionClass = sessionmaker(bind=engine)
session = SessionClass()
q = session.query(RdfsSubclassOfStatement)
q = q.add_entity(OwlSomeValuesFrom)
q = q.join(OwlSomeValuesFrom, RdfsSubclassOfStatement.object == OwlSomeValuesFrom.id)

lines = []
for ax, ex in q.all():
    line = f'{ax.subject} subClassOf {ex.on_property} SOME {ex.filler}'
    logging.info(line)
    lines.append(line)
```    

(this example is just for illustration - to do the same thing there is a simpler Edge relation)

## Applications

The semsql python library is intentionally low level - we recommend using the [ontology-access-kit](https://github.com/INCATools/ontology-access-kit)

For example:

```bash
runoak -i db/envo.db search t~biome
```

You can also pass in an OWL file and have the sqlite be made on the fly

```bash
runoak -i sqlite:envo.owl search t~biome
```

Even if using OAK, it can be useful to access SQL tables directly to do complex multi-join queries in a performant way.

## Optimization

```bash
poetry run semsql view2table edge --full-index | sqlite3 $db/mydb.db
```

See [indexes](indexes) for some ready-made indexes

functions.name: semsql.builder.cli.query
documentation:
Performs a SQL query on an OWL file

Example:

    semsql query -i hp.owl "SELECT * FROM rdfs_label_statement WHERE value LIKE 'Abnormality of %'"

functions.name: semsql.builder.cli.view2table
documentation:
Generates a command that turns a view into a table

Example usage:

    semsql view2table rdfs_label_statement --index | sqlite3 db/pato.db

functions.name: semsql.ontlib.common_queries.term_search
documentation:
Maps a list of terms (e.g. query search terms to match labels, or IDs) to a list of IDs

The intended use for this is to take a user query where a user may want to query
either by IDs, or to provide a list of exact terms (e.g. "forelimb"), or queries
(e.g. "%limb%"), and expand the term list to IDs

The caller can specify a view, which is the SQLAlchemy model class used in the query;
this should be a subtype of Statements.

If view is none, then the output simply matches the input (i.e the user knows IDs already)

```
param session



param terms

list of query terms

param view

view to use, e.g. RdfsLabelView

return

list of IDs from expansion
```

Gives a set of IDs, extract a subgraph using these IDs as seeds

Different view classes can be supplied; these map to SQL views conforming to the same structure

The default view is SubgraphEdgeByAncestor, which returns all edges whose subject is an ancestor
of a seed ID

functions.name: semsql.ontlib.subgraph.cli
documentation:
Extract subgraph

Examples:

 
    List all edges for a given term
        $ subgraph-d tests/inputs/go-nucleus.db CL:0000000
 
    List all edges for subjects whose label starts 'nucle' (e.g. nucleus)
        $ subgraph-d tests/inputs/go-nucleus.db -m label nucle%
 
    Visualize subgraph seeded from terms matching a label:
        $ subgraph-d tests/inputs/go-nucleus.db -m label nucle% -f viz
    (requires obographviz)
 
    As above, with stylesheet:
        $ subgraph-d tests/inputs/go-nucleus.db -m label nucle% -f viz -s builder/obograph-style.json
 
    As above, with additional stylesheet configuration, and filtering relations
        $ subgraph-d tests/inputs/go-nucleus.db -m label nucle% -f viz -p s,BFO:0000050             -s builder/obograph-style.json -C 'containmentRelations: [rdfs:subClassOf]'
 
    As above, but visualizing edges that are descendants of query
        $ subgraph-d tests/inputs/go-nucleus.db -V down -m label nucle% -f viz -s builder/obograph-style.json

classes.name: semsql.sqla.omo.OwlComplexAxiom
documentation: An axiom that is composed of two or more statements

classes.name: semsql.sqla.omo.Prefix
documentation: Maps CURIEs to URIs

classes.name: semsql.sqla.omo.Statements
documentation: Represents an RDF triple

classes.name: semsql.sqla.omo.Node
documentation: The basic unit of representation in an RDF or OWL graph

classes.name: semsql.sqla.omo.RdfLevelSummaryStatistic
documentation: Abstract grouping for views/classes that provide some kind of count summary about an individual element

classes.name: semsql.sqla.omo.OntologyNode
documentation: A node representing an ontology

classes.name: semsql.sqla.omo.OwlReifiedAxiom
documentation: An OWL axiom that has been reified - i.e. it includes an [id](id) field that uniquely identifies that axiom and which can be the subject of additional statements

classes.name: semsql.sqla.omo.OwlSubclassOfSomeValuesFrom
documentation: Composition of subClassOf and SomeValuesFrom

classes.name: semsql.sqla.omo.OwlEquivalentToIntersectionMember
documentation: Composition of `OwlEquivalentClass`, `OwlIntersectionOf`, and `RdfListMember`; `C = X1 and ... and Xn`

classes.name: semsql.sqla.omo.NodeToNodeStatement
documentation: A statement where object is non-null and value is not populated

classes.name: semsql.sqla.omo.NodeToValueStatement
documentation: A statement where value is non-null and object is not populated

classes.name: semsql.sqla.omo.RdfListStatement
documentation: A statement that is used to represent aspects of RDF lists

classes.name: semsql.sqla.omo.BlankNode
documentation: A node with an ID that is not preserved between databases

classes.name: semsql.sqla.omo.ClassNode
documentation: A node that represents an RDFS/OWL class

classes.name: semsql.sqla.omo.PropertyNode
documentation: Note this only directly classifies nodes asserted to be rdf:Properties

classes.name: semsql.sqla.omo.NamedIndividualNode
documentation: A node that represents an OWL Named Individual

classes.name: semsql.sqla.omo.CountOfPredicates
documentation: Number of distinct usages of a predicate. NOTE MAY CHANGE: does not currently count existential usage in OWL

classes.name: semsql.sqla.omo.CountOfInstantiatedClasses
documentation: Number of distinct instantiations of a class. Note in many OBOs, classes are not directly instantiated

classes.name: semsql.sqla.omo.CountOfSubclasses
documentation: Number of subclasses for a given class

classes.name: semsql.sqla.omo.ObjectPropertyNode
documentation: A node representing an OWL object property

classes.name: semsql.sqla.omo.AnnotationPropertyNode
documentation: A node representing an OWL annotation property

classes.name: semsql.sqla.omo.OwlEquivalentClassStatement
documentation: A statement that connects two class_nodes where both classes are equivalent

classes.name: semsql.sqla.omo.OwlSameAsStatement
documentation: A statement that connects two individual nodes where both individual are equivalent

classes.name: semsql.sqla.omo.AnonymousExpression
documentation: An OWL expression, such as a class expression. Expressions are "anonymous" as they are a composition of named elements rather than a named element themselves

classes.name: semsql.sqla.omo.RdfTypeStatement
documentation: A statement that indicates the asserted type of the subject entity

classes.name: semsql.sqla.omo.RdfFirstStatement
documentation: A statement that connects a list to its first element. This is a low-level triple, it is unlikely you need to use this directly. It is used to define rdf_list_member_statement, which is more useful

classes.name: semsql.sqla.omo.RdfRestStatement
documentation: A statement that connects a list to its remaining elements. This is a low-level triple, it is unlikely you need to use this directly. It is used to define rdf_list_member_statement, which is more useful

classes.name: semsql.sqla.omo.RdfListNode
documentation: A node representing an RDF list. Note that you will not likely need to use this directly.

classes.name: semsql.sqla.omo.TransitivePropertyNode
documentation: A node representing an OWL transitive object property

classes.name: semsql.sqla.omo.SymmetricPropertyNode
documentation: A node representing an OWL symmetric object property

classes.name: semsql.sqla.omo.ReflexivePropertyNode
documentation: A node representing an OWL reflexive object property

classes.name: semsql.sqla.omo.IrreflexivePropertyNode
documentation: A node representing an OWL irreflexive object property

classes.name: semsql.sqla.omo.AnonymousClassExpression
documentation: An OWL anonymous class expression, such as for example `SomeValuesFrom(partOf Hand)`

classes.name: semsql.sqla.omo.OwlRestriction
documentation: An OWL restriction, such as `SomeValuesFrom(partOf Hand)`

classes.name: semsql.sqla.omo.OwlSomeValuesFrom
documentation: An OWL SomeValuesFrom restriction

classes.name: semsql.sqla.obo.RepairAction

classes.name: semsql.sqla.obo.Problem

classes.name: semsql.sqla.obo.ObjectPropertyNode
documentation: A node representing an OWL object property

classes.name: semsql.sqla.obo.AnnotationPropertyNode
documentation: A node representing an OWL annotation property

classes.name: semsql.sqla.obo.OwlEquivalentClassStatement
documentation: A statement that connects two class_nodes where both classes are equivalent

classes.name: semsql.sqla.obo.OwlSameAsStatement
documentation: A statement that connects two individual nodes where both individual are equivalent

classes.name: semsql.sqla.obo.AnonymousExpression
documentation: An OWL expression, such as a class expression. Expressions are "anonymous" as they are a composition of named elements rather than a named element themselves

classes.name: semsql.sqla.obo.RdfTypeStatement
documentation: A statement that indicates the asserted type of the subject entity

classes.name: semsql.sqla.obo.RdfFirstStatement
documentation: A statement that connects a list to its first element. This is a low-level triple, it is unlikely you need to use this directly. It is used to define rdf_list_member_statement, which is more useful

classes.name: semsql.sqla.obo.RdfRestStatement
documentation: A statement that connects a list to its remaining elements. This is a low-level triple, it is unlikely you need to use this directly. It is used to define rdf_list_member_statement, which is more useful

classes.name: semsql.sqla.obo.RdfListNode
documentation: A node representing an RDF list. Note that you will not likely need to use this directly.

classes.name: semsql.sqla.obo.TransitivePropertyNode
documentation: A node representing an OWL transitive object property

classes.name: semsql.sqla.obo.SymmetricPropertyNode
documentation: A node representing an OWL symmetric object property

classes.name: semsql.sqla.obo.ReflexivePropertyNode
documentation: A node representing an OWL reflexive object property

classes.name: semsql.sqla.obo.IrreflexivePropertyNode
documentation: A node representing an OWL irreflexive object property

classes.name: semsql.sqla.obo.AnonymousClassExpression
documentation: An OWL anonymous class expression, such as for example `SomeValuesFrom(partOf Hand)`

classes.name: semsql.sqla.obo.OwlRestriction
documentation: An OWL restriction, such as `SomeValuesFrom(partOf Hand)`

classes.name: semsql.sqla.obo.OwlSomeValuesFrom
documentation: An OWL SomeValuesFrom restriction

classes.name: semsql.sqla.semsql.TermAssociation
documentation: A minimal datamodel for relating a subject entity to an object term

classes.name: semsql.sqla.semsql.OwlComplexAxiom
documentation: An axiom that is composed of two or more statements

classes.name: semsql.sqla.semsql.Prefix
documentation: Maps CURIEs to URIs

classes.name: semsql.sqla.semsql.Statements
documentation: Represents an RDF triple

classes.name: semsql.sqla.semsql.Node
documentation: The basic unit of representation in an RDF or OWL graph

classes.name: semsql.sqla.semsql.RdfLevelSummaryStatistic
documentation: Abstract grouping for views/classes that provide some kind of count summary about an individual element

classes.name: semsql.sqla.semsql.RelationGraphConstruct
documentation: A construct used as part of a Relation Graph

classes.name: semsql.sqla.semsql.RepairAction
documentation: Represents an action that needs to be taken to repair a problem

classes.name: semsql.sqla.semsql.Problem
documentation: Represents an instance of a problem pertaining to conformance to OBO guidelines

classes.name: semsql.sqla.semsql.OntologyNode
documentation: A node representing an ontology

classes.name: semsql.sqla.semsql.OwlReifiedAxiom
documentation: An OWL axiom that has been reified - i.e. it includes an [id](id) field that uniquely identifies that axiom and which can be the subject of additional statements

classes.name: semsql.sqla.semsql.OwlSubclassOfSomeValuesFrom
documentation: Composition of subClassOf and SomeValuesFrom

classes.name: semsql.sqla.semsql.OwlEquivalentToIntersectionMember
documentation: Composition of `OwlEquivalentClass`, `OwlIntersectionOf`, and `RdfListMember`; `C = X1 and ... and Xn`

classes.name: semsql.sqla.semsql.NodeToNodeStatement
documentation: A statement where object is non-null and value is not populated

classes.name: semsql.sqla.semsql.NodeToValueStatement
documentation: A statement where value is non-null and object is not populated

classes.name: semsql.sqla.semsql.RdfListStatement
documentation: A statement that is used to represent aspects of RDF lists

classes.name: semsql.sqla.semsql.BlankNode
documentation: A node with an ID that is not preserved between databases

classes.name: semsql.sqla.semsql.ClassNode
documentation: A node that represents an RDFS/OWL class

classes.name: semsql.sqla.semsql.PropertyNode
documentation: Note this only directly classifies nodes asserted to be rdf:Properties

classes.name: semsql.sqla.semsql.NamedIndividualNode
documentation: A node that represents an OWL Named Individual

classes.name: semsql.sqla.semsql.CountOfPredicates
documentation: Number of distinct usages of a predicate. NOTE MAY CHANGE: does not currently count existential usage in OWL

classes.name: semsql.sqla.semsql.CountOfInstantiatedClasses
documentation: Number of distinct instantiations of a class. Note in many OBOs, classes are not directly instantiated

classes.name: semsql.sqla.semsql.CountOfSubclasses
documentation: Number of subclasses for a given class

classes.name: semsql.sqla.semsql.Edge
documentation: A relation graph edge that connects two entities by a predicate. Note an edge is distinct from a statement, in that an axiom such as A SubClassOf R some B is represented as multiple statements, but is a single relation graph edge

classes.name: semsql.sqla.semsql.SubgraphQuery
documentation: A subgraph query encompasses as subgraph edge and a seed/anchor object and seed/anchor predicate

classes.name: semsql.sqla.semsql.EntailedEdge
documentation: A relation graph edge that is inferred

classes.name: semsql.sqla.semsql.EntailedEdgeCycle
documentation: An edge that composes with another edge to make a cycle

classes.name: semsql.sqla.semsql.TransitiveEdge
documentation: A relation graph edge that is formed from a chain of one or more edges

classes.name: semsql.sqla.semsql.LexicalProblem
documentation: a problem with the textual value of an annotation property

classes.name: semsql.sqla.semsql.PropertyUsedWithDatatypeValuesAndObjects
documentation: A problem in which the same property is used two two different ways, one in which the range is a literal value, the other where it is an object.

documentation: A node representing an OWL object property

documentation: An edge within a subgraph anchored around a set of ancestor terms
